2004-04-01.title: To Portray The Horror, News Media Agonize
2004-04-01.abstract: Photographs and videotaped images of attack on four American security workers in Falluja present editors and producers with challenge: how to show what happened without offending viewers and readers; most large newspapers decide that horror of events could not be fully conveyed without printing some of more grisly images; on television, where impact of images might be more powerful, no consensus emerges; most grisly images appear on Internet, sometimes accompanied by viewer warnings (M)

2004-04-01.title: Day After Grisly Killings, Iraqi Newspapers Look Elsewhere
2004-04-01.abstract: At least four of the main Iraqi newspapers led their front pages with stories that would directly affect the lives of their readers.

2004-04-01.title: Hong Kong Protest Opposes Potential Curbs on Democracy
2004-04-01.abstract: A couple thousand people held a vigil in Hong Kong today to protest Beijings plans to restrict Hong Kongs movement toward greater democracy.

2004-04-01.title: Europeans Criticize Iran's Plan To Start Up Enrichment Plant
2004-04-01.abstract: Foreign ministries of Germany, France and Britain criticize Iran's decision to start up uranium conversion plant in Isfahan; move comes after much-heralded diplomatic initiative by three countries that resulted last Oct in promise by Iran to suspend its uranium enrichment activities and to allow more intrusive international inspections of its nuclear program (M)

2004-04-01.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Saudi Arabia: Cleric Condemns TV Show
2004-04-01.abstract: Saudi Arabian lead cleric Grand Mufti Sheik Abdulaziz bin Abdullah al-Sheik proclaims that Star Academy, Arabic television show, goes against teachings of Islam (S)

2004-04-02.title: World Briefing: Asia
2004-04-02.abstract: NEPAL: Thousands Protest Against King; UZBEKISTAN: Violence Persists;.

2004-04-02.title: Britain Extends Its Detention of 8 Bomb Suspects
2004-04-02.abstract: Scotland Yard antiterrorism branch says it has extended to Apr 3 the detention of eight men arrested in connection with seizure of 1,000 pounds of bomb-making compound near central London; says arrests in Canada and Saudi Arabia of father and son from Pakistan may be connected to investigation into suspected bomb-making activities in London; police say no detonators, timing devices or other paraphernalia was discovered in raid (M)

2004-04-02.title: How Trickle of AIDS Drugs Can Bring a Torrent of Hope
2004-04-02.abstract: Free antiretroviral drugs become available to treat people in late stages of AIDS in Gauteng Province in South Africa, after years of political debate and threatened lawsuit; experts say there are as many as 1.6 million HIV-positive people in Gauteng, and that without antiretrovirals, death would be looming for about 164,000 of them; Pres Thabo Mbeki and his health minister persistently have questioned whether virus causes AIDS and whether antiretroviral drugs help; Mbeki promised to provide drugs free under enormous political and diplomatic pressure (M)

2004-04-02.title: Britain Cracks Down on Nasties Like the 'Neighbor From Hell'
2004-04-02.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair's government has enacted array of measures since 1999 to confront what is widely seen as erosion of civilized norms in Britain; about 1,600 Britons have been served so-called 'antisocial behavior orders' as part of aggressive effort by state to police behavior that would once have been purview of families or neighborhoods; authorities can fine or jail parents of children who are chronic truants, impose on-the-spot fines for defacing public property and evict 'neighbors from hell' from public housing; recipients of antisoical orders can be banned from entering certain neighborhoods or hanging out with particualr group of people--even from wearing certain clothes or visiting members of their own families; critics say new antisocial orders stigmatize and marginalize population that is already on fringes of society, leaving offenders with litle hope of improvement or rehabilitation; Home Office, in 24-hour experiment, recorded 60,000 instances of antisocial behavior; some examples cited; many in government attribute rise in antisocial behavior to emergence of social security-dependent culture that promotes feeling of entitlement but not feeling of responsibility; photo (M)

2004-04-02.title: Manila arrests 4 Turkish suspects
2004-04-02.abstract: MANILA:—  Four Turkish nationals working as teachers were arrested in a southern Philippine city on suspicion that they were involved in terrorist activities, officials said Thursday.

2004-04-03.title: Fears rise as bomb is found on Spanish rail
2004-04-03.abstract: MADRID:—  Less than a month after the deadly train bombings here, a bomb was found Friday under the tracks of a high-speed train link between Madrid and Seville, raising fears that Spain is the target for further terrorist attacks and prompting the police to raise the alert level.

2004-04-03.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Tiananmen Relatives Freed
2004-04-03.abstract: Zhang Xianling, Ding Zilin and Huang Jinping, who are members of protest group Tiananmen Mothers and were arrested last week, are released from Chinese custody after domestic and international calls for freedom; had given videotaped testimony about 1989 army crackdown in Beijing that was to be presented at United Nations conference; some hold arrests are part of increased security as fifteenth anniversary of event approaches (S)

2004-04-03.title: U.S. takes opposite tack from France in head scarf debate
2004-04-03.abstract: WASHINGTON:—  The hallways of Benjamin Franklin Science Academy in quiet Muskogee, Oklahoma, are far from those of the tough, lower-class Paris suburbs where the battle was fought over the right of Muslim girls to wear head scarves required by their religion.

2004-04-03.title: U.N. Aide Says Sudan Is Tolerating Ethnic Cleansing
2004-04-03.abstract: Jan Egeland, United Nations director for relief in Sudan, says Arab militias are conducting campaign of ethnic cleansing to drive black Africans out of border region of country with apparent tolerance of Sudanese government; estimates that 750,000 people have been forced from their homes, tens of thousands have fled into Chad and 10,000 have died (M)

2004-04-03.title: Globalist : The Republicans' barb:John Kerry 'looks French'
2004-04-03.abstract: PARIS—  It is not going to be a pretty American election. Already the Bush administration has embarked on a campaign to portray John Kerry as a flip-flopping, tax-raising, European-educated wimp. The presumptive Democratic candidate has responded by describing the president as a job-destroying, budget-busting, alliance-breaking unilateralist.

2004-04-04.title: Bosnian Jew Promotes Inquiry Into Causes of the 1990's War
2004-04-04.abstract: Jacob Finci, leader of Sarajevo's Jews and director of Bosnian civil service agency, hopes to form commission to investigate causes of 1990's war in Bosnia that killed 200,000 and displaced more than one million people; is seen as fair arbiter between Muslims, Serbs and Croats; Finci hopes to base commission loosely on model of South Africa's Truth and Reconciliation Commission, but has met with little enthusiasm from any of involved parties (M)

2004-04-04.title: Political Rivals Nip at Governing South African Party
2004-04-04.abstract: African National Congress is expected to win two-thirds of vote in South Africa's third post-apartheid national election on April 14, as it did two previous elections, but Patricia De Lille's upstart Independent Democratic Party hopes to cut into landslide; De Lille's year-old party exploits combination of rising disaffection with ANC and decline of squabbling opposition parties; De Lille is former anti-apartheid activist, current member of Parliament and unsparing critic of ANC; her party hopes to win five to ten percent of vote in upcoming parliamentary elections; photo (M)

2004-04-04.title: Saving Iraq's Archaeological Past From Thieves Remains an Uphill Battle
2004-04-04.abstract: Thieves are busy looting countless archaeological sites in Iraq as protecting antiquities is low priority for Iraqi and coalition authorities; Carabinieri serving with Italian contingent in Iraq, are only authorities making sustained effort to protect antiquities; have mapped local sites in Dhi Qar Province south of Baghdad, arrested dozens of looters and recovered more than 400 objects; Italians are setting up program to train and equip Iraqi security guards to patrol all archaeological sites; photos, map (M)

2004-04-04.title: Names of the Dead
2004-04-04.abstract: Defense Department confirms death of one American soldier in Iraq, bringing total number of American service members killed since start of Iraq war to 600 (S)

2004-04-04.title: South Korean Conservatives Try to Regain Voter Confidence
2004-04-04.abstract: South Korean conservatives are desperate to redeem themselves after leading unpopular vote to impeach Pres Roh Moo Hyun; face crucial parliamentary elections April 15; electorate is split along generational lines; younger voters favor liberals, while older voters favor conservatives; Roh is 13 months into five-year term and is not up for election; is expected to survive impeachment attempt with strong parliamentary support, allowing him to steer course more independent of United States and more accommodating toward China and North Korea; photo (M)

2004-04-05.title: Huge Basque Arsenal Found in Southwestern France
2004-04-05.abstract: Interior Min Angel Acebes of Spain announces that police have discovered large arsenal of Basque separatist group ETA in southwestern France; says site contained grenades, other weapons and explosives, and may also have been arms factory; says French police raid also resulted in arrests of two people suspected of being ETA members (S)

2004-04-05.title: After the Battle, Mourning and Anger in an Iraqi Slum
2004-04-05.abstract: Almost one year ago, the Shiites of Sadr City were throwing flowers at the American tanks. But today, the Iraqis were filling plain wood coffins with the corpses of their kin.

2004-04-05.title: 7 U.S. Soldiers Die in Iraq as a Shiite Militia Rises Up
2004-04-05.abstract: Co-ordinated Shiite militia uprising against American-led occupation reaches into Baghdad's sprawling Shiite slum of Sadr City, where seven American soldiers are killed, and roils holy city of Najaf and at least two other cities in southern Iraq; Iraqi health official in Najaf says 24 people were killed and 200 wounded in clashes that ensued when armed militiamen loyal to firebrand Shiite cleric Moktada al-Sadr besieged garrison commanded by Spanish troops on road leading into Najaf; tens of thousands of militiamen emerge into streets of Baghdad, Najaf, Kufa and Amara hours after Sadr tells his followers to 'terrorize your enemy'; armed militiamen easily take control of checkpoints and police stations in Sadr City, Najaf and Kufa that were staffed by new Iraqi-trained police and civil defense force; residents say Iraqis abandoned their posts as soon as militiamen appeared, punching hole in American hopes that American-trained Iraqis can take over providing security in major cities; until now, powerful Shiite clerics have largely avoided pitting their private militias against American-led occupation forces, preferring to challenge Americans politically; Grand Ayatollah Ali al-Sistani has urged followers to protest peacefully, but Sadr has been menacing presence in shadows since onset of American occupation; photos; map (L)

2004-04-05.title: Names of the Dead
2004-04-05.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of service member in Iraq (S)

2004-04-05.title: That Came From Us
2004-04-05.abstract: Information provided by the German Federal Intelligence Service on Iraqi biological weapons has become a political issue: Did the Germans, by providing incorrect information, inadvertently contribute to the outbreak of the war?

2004-04-06.title: On Visit to Haiti, Powell Says the U.S. Weighs Prosecution of Aristide on Corruption Charges
2004-04-06.abstract: Secretary of State Colin L Powell, in Haiti, says American judicial authorities may prosecute former Pres Jean-Bertrand Aristide on corruption charges; says he opposes request by 15-nation Caribbean Community for United Nations to investigate terms of Aristide's departure from Haiti; United States, along with France, Canada and Chile, has sent troops to Haiti to help maintain order; Bush administration officials are trying to establish security and bolster authority of government of interim Prime Minister Gerard Latortue; they are displeased that Aristide has returned to region, as guest of government of Jamaica; photo (M)

2004-04-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Aznar To Teach At Georgetown
2004-04-06.abstract: Former Spanish Prime Minister Jose Maria Aznar will teach at Georgetown University (Washington, DC) in fall 2004; photo (S)

2004-04-06.title: U.S. SEEKS ARREST OF SHIITE CLERIC
2004-04-06.abstract: American officials in Iraq confirm they have issued arrest warrant for Moktada al-Sadr, militant Shiite cleric whose followers started uprising in six Iraqi cities over weekend; Sadr says he would 'welcome' showdown with American forces; has pledged to drive them out of Iraq; warrant for his arrest is issued in connection with shooting and hacking to death of rival Shiite cleric at mosque in Najaf last Apr; announcement, months after warrant was secretly approved, seems timed to coincide with new American military offensive; American troops appear to be moving into area around Kufa, where Sadr's followers have seized control and cleric has taken refuge in heavily guarded mosque; marines move into Falluja, where four American security guards working for occupation authority were killed in ambush and then mutilated last week; American officials are taking tough stance instead of seeking, as they have in past, to avoid confrontation that might fan antagonism for Americans; they appear determined to try to seize Sadr; American administrator L Paul Bremer II calls him an outlaw; photo; maps (L)

2004-04-06.title: World Briefing | Americas: Canada: Slaughter Ordered For 19 Million Poultry
2004-04-06.abstract: British Columbia (Canada) Agriculture Minister Bob Speller mandates slaughter of 19 million chickens, turkeys and ducks in effort to halt spread of avian influenza; estimated 80 percent of province's poultry industry will be destroyed (S)

2004-04-06.title: Beijing Asserts New Control Over Election Laws in Hong Kong
2004-04-06.abstract: Standing Committee of China's National People's Congress approves new rules interpreting how far Hong Kong can go in pursuing greater democracy; committee official says Hong Kong government is required to work with congress before making any changes regarding how leaders are chosen, but that changes can be made 'if necessary' before next chief executive is chosen in 2007; Hong Kong's mini-constitution says chief executives elected after 2007 can be elected by more democratic means; current laws only allow committee of prominent citizens, mostly loyal to Beijing, to select candidates (M)

2004-04-07.title: China Is Paying a Price of Modernization: More Beggars
2004-04-07.abstract: Number of beggars in Beijing and other Chinese cities has exploded in past six months; their presence, long blocked by image-conscious city officials, is another reminder of growing divide between rich and poor as China rapidly switches to market economy; is also unexpected result of major civil rights victory last year, curb on police arrest powers; raises question of what should be done about beggars; some urban residents cite need to remedy core problem of rural poverty; photo (M)

2004-04-07.title: Afghan Army Sending Troops to Conflict-Ridden North
2004-04-07.abstract: For the second time in two weeks President Hamid Karzai is sending newly trained Afghan National Army troops from the capital to quell fighting in outlying regions.

2004-04-07.title: Lithuanian Parliament Removes Country's President After Casting Votes on Three Charges
2004-04-07.abstract: Lithuanian Parliament votes to oust Pres Rolandas Paksas, finding he violated his oath and Constitution through dealings with Yuri Borisov, businessman suspected of links to Russian organized crime; Parliament speaker, Arturas Paulauskas, takes over as acting president, pending new election to be held within few months; Paksas admits mistakes but holds none merited impeachment; photo (M)

2004-04-07.title: Hong Kong Reformers Protest Election Rule
2004-04-07.abstract: Hong Kong democracy advocates plan march to protest Chinese government's ruling that it will determine if and when territory's people can elect their local leaders; decision, delivered as interpretation of Hong Kong's charter by China's Communist Party-controlled Congress, crushes hopes that Hong Kong can chart its own course toward democracy; opposition parties say ruling infringes on 50-year period of autonomy China promised when it resumed sovereignty; photo (M)

2004-04-07.title: Uneasy Greeks Focus on Olympic Safety
2004-04-07.abstract: Greek authorities, already on defensive because of delays in completing sporting sites for Athens Summer Olympics, are confronting renewed fears about security at Games; have asked help of NATO, which is expected to deploy ships and Awac surveillance planes to patrol coastline and provide other help to protect against terrorist attacks; Greek officials note they are spending nearly $1 billion on security operation that is by far costliest and most extensive in Olympic history (M)

2004-04-08.title: Philippine Militant and 5 Followers Are Killed in Firefight
2004-04-08.abstract: A leader of the Muslim militant group Abu Sayyaf and five of his men were killed during a firefight with government troops on a southern Philippine island today, officials said.

2004-04-08.title: Asia Letter : Indonesian Islamist party is quietly gaining ground
2004-04-08.abstract: JAKARTA—  Clean. Healthy. Caring. That was the slogan of the highly tuned Justice and Prosperity Party, a religious Islamic party led by well-educated men and women that performed surprisingly well in Indonesia's parliamentary elections on Monday.

2004-04-08.title: Russian Court Gives Scientist 15-Year Sentence for Spying for U.S.
2004-04-08.abstract: Russian arms control expert Igor Sutyagin is sentenced in Moscow to 15 years at hard labor for spying for United States; rights groups say case harks back to Soviet-style repression; he was found guilty of espionage for selling to British company information that he and fellow scientists say was unclassified and open to public; prosecutors claim company was cover for Central Intelligence Agency; his is latest in string of cases that appear to reflect concerns within Russian security services about contacts between Russian and foreign scientists; FSB, successor to KGB, has stepped up investigations and prosecutions of scientists since election of Pres Putin, who formerly headed agency (M)

2004-04-08.title: Germans Free Moroccan Convicted of a 9/11 Role
2004-04-08.abstract: German court releases Mounir el-Motassadeq, only man convicted in connection with 9/11 terrorist attacks in United States, pending new trial later this year; his release follows decision by German appeals court to reverse his conviction, ruling that he had been denied fair trial because of refusal of US to allow testimony by captured terrorist suspect Ramzi bin al-Shibh; Motassadeq appealed his conviction after another 9/11 suspect Abdelghani Mzoudi was acquitted when US refused to permit bin al-Shibh to testify at his trial; Motassadeq's case is sure to present same legal issues that resulted in collapse of case against Mzoudi (M)

2004-04-08.title: New Zealand Politics Roiled by Indigenous Rights, Marital Sanctity and Agnosticism
2004-04-08.abstract: Roiling political storm has been whipped up in New Zealand by Donald Brash, leader of conservative National Party who claims government has gone too far in apologizing and giving special privileges to Maori, native population that lost its land and culture when first Europeans arrived; says there should be no special privileges for any race; his supporters see his stance as being against political correctness, while critics see it as racism; in recent years Maori have invoked 1840 treaty to gain tens of millions of dollars in compensation for land taken from them and to assert wide-ranging right to seabeds and fisheries; Brash appears to have tapped into pent up lingering resentment among many New Zealand voters (M),

2004-04-09.title: IRAQ
2004-04-09.abstract: ANGUISH OVER HOSTAGES -- Japan woke up today to televised scenes of the human price of the deployment of its soldiers to Iraq.   PAGE A8    ARAB CRITICISM OF U.S. -- Some Arabs expressed the fear that the United States might be incubating extremism in Iraq.   PAGE A10

2004-04-09.title: Suutarila Journal; Educators Flocking to Finland, Land of Literate Children
2004-04-09.abstract: Educators from all over world are flocking to Finland to deconstruct its school system ever since Finnish schools topped respected international survey, coming in first in literacy and placing in top five in math and science; survey was conducted by Orgn of Economic Cooperation and Development; photo (M)

2004-04-09.title: Marines Battle Guerrillas in Streets of Falluja
2004-04-09.abstract: More than 1,200 marines, backed by attack helicopters and fighter-bombers, battle insurgents in fierce block-by-block fighting for fourth straight day in Falluja, epicenter of Sunni resistance; American warplanes drop two laser-guided bombs on houses where insurgents are thought to have taken cover; military spokesman says marines and their Iraqi allies control about one-quarter of Falluja; photo (M)

2004-04-09.title: Security Firm Says Its Workers Were Lured Into Iraqi Ambush
2004-04-09.abstract: Four private security contractors killed, burned and mutilated in Falluja last week were reportedly lured into carefully planned ambush by men purporting to be friendly members of Iraqi Civil Defense Corps; investigation by Blackwater USA, victims' employer, has not yet determined whether Blackwater employees were led into Falluja by active members of Iraqi Civil Defense Corps, or by imposters wearing defense corps uniforms; Blackwater official notes that imposters or not, incident underscores deepening concern about reliability of Iraqi civil defense forces at time when allied troops are fighting in many parts of Iraq to suppress militant Sunni and Shiite groups; Pentagon says it cannot confirm conclusions of Blackwater investigation and is conducting separate military inquiry; Blackwater's account, if confirmed, could deflect blame for incident from company (M)

2004-04-09.title: China's Premier Orders Halt to a Dam Project Threatening a Lost Eden
2004-04-09.abstract: Prime Min Wen Jiabao unexpectedly suspends plans for massive dam system on Nu River in western China that scientists have warned could ruin one of country's last unspoiled places; most dam opponents had considered project a fait accompli; Wen's personal involvement is rare response in non-democratic government that in past has shown little concern about environmental effect of major public works projects; Wen has ordered officials to conduct major review of hydropower project; photo (M)

2004-04-10.title: U.S. Relies on U.N. to Solve Problems of Power Transfer
2004-04-10.abstract: State Dept dismisses idea that increased fighting in Iraq might force delay in planned transfer of power to Iraqis by June 30; senior Bush administration officials, in light of anti-US insurrection, say US is relying increasingly on United Nations to put international stamp on efforts to resolve differences among Iraqis on makeup of interim government (M)

2004-04-10.title: Fighting Halts Briefly in Falluja; U.S. Convoy Hit Near Baghdad
2004-04-10.abstract: US cease-fire briefly halts fighting in Falluja that has created worst crisis of US occupation and left hundreds of Iraqis and at least 40 Americans dead; US officials say cease-fire was ordered by occupation authority head L Paul Bremer III; two US soldiers and unknown number of civilian contractors are missing after their convoy is attacked on highway near Falluja; senior Iraqi officials Abdul Karim al-Muhammadawi and Abdul Basit Turki resign from Governing Council in protest over Falluja offensive; cease-fire is broken almost immediately by bursts of gunfire and at least one US air strike; US command warns that full-scale offensive will resume if talks falter; photos (M)

2004-04-10.title: World Briefing | Asia: Georgia: Province Hands Over Prisoner
2004-04-10.abstract: Adzharia (Georgia) Province leader Aslan Abashidze, responding to ruling by European Court of Human Rights, releases political prisoner Tengiz Asanidze; President Mikhail Saakashvili pressures province to disarm local renegade forces (S)

2004-04-10.title: Algerian President Overwhelmingly Wins Re-election
2004-04-10.abstract: Algerian Pres Abdelaziz Bouteflika wins second term in landslide vote as voters reward him for bringing relative peace to country weary from over decade of civil war; frustrated opposition candidates say fraud played role in Bouteflika's victory, in which he received 83.5 percent of vote, and they vow to contest election; 130 international observers approve results; photo (M)

2004-04-10.title: China's Martha Stewart, With Reasons to Smile
2004-04-10.abstract: Profile of Zhou Zhu, host of popular television show in China that offers tips on Western-style middle-class life style; photo (M)

2004-04-11.title: Caste System Binds Nepalese Prostitutes
2004-04-11.abstract: Caste has become destiny for many communities, but few people have inherited so vexed a destiny as the Badis of Nepal, who work as prostitutes.

2004-04-11.title: Hong Kong Marchers Send Beijing a Message on Democracy
2004-04-11.abstract: After a large but violent demonstration in Taipei on Saturday over an election dispute, today's rally was another reminder for Beijing.

2004-04-11.title: Billions of Trees Planted, and Nary a Dent in the Desert
2004-04-11.abstract: China has planted 42 billion trees since 1982 in effort to hem in Taklimakan Desert; announced 10-year plan in 2002 to plant trees over 170,000 square miles; United Nations statistics show increase in average number of sandstorms from 5 in 1960's to 24 in 1990's; Chinese government reports desert is growing at rate of 3,800 square miles per year; no reckoning of tree project's cost or effectiveness has been carried out, but huge cost will be financial disaster if effort is not effective; photos; map (M)

2004-04-11.title: Names of the Dead
2004-04-11.abstract: Defense Department confirms death of one American service member in Iraq, bringing total to 641 dead (S)

2004-04-11.title: Blair Stands Firm With Bush in Face of New Iraq Fighting
2004-04-11.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair committed himself anew today to the war in Iraq, calling it a "historic struggle" against dictatorship, terrorism and fanaticism across the globe.

2004-04-12.title: Souvenance Journal; An Easter Voodoo Festival With Political Undertones
2004-04-12.abstract: Hundreds of Haitians flock to tiny village of Souvenance to sacred temple where Haitians have for last 200 years been showing their devotion to African spirits brought to island by slaves; it is one of holiest pilgrimages for believers in voodoo and also first major pilgrimage since Pres Jean-Bertrand Aristide was pushed from power by armed uprising; representatives from new government and rebel soldiers who helped push Aristide from power come to temple to pay their respects; photo; map (M)

2004-04-12.title: On Easter Sunday in Texas, Bush Awards Purple Hearts
2004-04-12.abstract: Pres Bush emerges from seclusion at his Texas ranch, attending Easter services at nearby Fort Hood and visiting military hospital to award Purple Hearts to 10 soldiers wounded in Iraq; this is his first public appearance through tumultuous week of news; he tells reporters this has been 'tough week' in Iraq (M)

2004-04-12.title: Dutch Worker In Chechnya Is Released After 2 Years
2004-04-12.abstract: Dutch aid worker Arjan Erkel, kidnapped almost two years ago near lawless region of Chechnya, is freed in police operation after months of failed attempts to negotiate his release; is with Doctors Without Borders; group had earlier accused Russian authorities of being involved in his kidnapping as warning to other aid groups to stay out of dangerous region; Foreign Min Bernard Bot of Netherlands says his government was instrumental in Erkel's release; photo (M)

2004-04-12.title: Bush, With Mubarak, Links Progress in Iraq to Mideast Peace
2004-04-12.abstract: The visit by Egypt's leader came at a delicate time, and at a confluence of several thorny issues.

2004-04-12.title: Blair Defends the War in Iraq As Part of a 'Historic Struggle'
2004-04-12.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair, preparing for meeting with Pres Bush, commits himself anew to war in Iraq, calling it 'historic struggle'; remarks are Blair's first public comments in week of intense conflict in Iraq pitting United States and allied forces against insurgents from both Sunni minority and Shiite majority; Blair's comments seek to link recent fighting with broader campaign against terrorism, even though his own Foreign Secretary Jack Straw has characterized insurgents as 'Iraqis, not foreign fighters' (M)

2004-04-13.title: South Africa's Ruling Party Expected to Dominate Vote
2004-04-13.abstract: When South Africans go to the polls Wednesday as many as seven in 10 will vote for the African National Congress.

2004-04-13.title: Members of Iraqi Council Bitter At Being Left Out of War Plans
2004-04-13.abstract: Members of Iraqi Governing Council, picked by United States to serve as transitional authority, say they were never consulted over large-scale American military moves last week and would have opposed decision to confront Sunni insurgents in Falluja and Ramadi; after members register their complaints, council sends delegation that negotiates cease-fire, becoming involved politically in talks to try to ease tensions in southern Shiite cities; Lt Gen Ricardo S Sanchez says US froze its military operations at governing council's request to allow for cease-fire discussions and aid deliveries to Falluja (M)

2004-04-13.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Top Court Orders Retrial For Hindus
2004-04-13.abstract: Indian Supreme Court orders retrial of 21 Hindus acquitted of charges that they burned alive 14 people during 2002 Gujarat riots; trial to be moved from Gujarat to Maharashtra State (S)

2004-04-13.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: Taliban Say They Killed Spy Chief
2004-04-13.abstract: Taliban spokesman reports that group has killed Uruzgan Province (Afghanistan) intelligence chief and two bodyguards kidnapped several days ago (S)

2004-04-13.title: Diplomacy Continues; Bodies Found May Be Missing Civilians
2004-04-13.abstract: As Iraqi envoys pursued diplomacy, there were reports that the bodies of four U.S. civilian workers may have been found.

2004-04-14.title: MORE ON IRAQ
2004-04-14.abstract: A CLERIC REMAINS DEFIANT -- A rebel cleric resisted disbanding his militia as American troops moved in to confront him, but there were reports a deal had been struck.   PAGE A10    ENVOY TO IRAQ TO BE NAMED -- John D. Negroponte, a veteran American diplomat, is expected to be appointed ambassador to Iraq.   PAGE A11

2004-04-14.title: Arroyo Says Philippines May Pull Out of Iraq
2004-04-14.abstract: President Gloria Macapagal Arroyo suggested today that she might withdraw Filipino military troops stationed in Iraq if the situation there deteriorates.

2004-04-14.title: In Beijing, Cheney Is Urged to Reduce Taiwan Military Support
2004-04-14.abstract: Vice Pres Dick Cheney, in Beijing for round of talks with China's top leaders, is urged to reduce United States arms sales to Taiwan to lessen risk of confict across Taiwan strait; American and Chinese officials say that talks will focus mainly on Taiwan and on North Korea's nuclear program (M)

2004-04-14.title: Voting in South Africa on Path to Expected Result
2004-04-14.abstract: When South Africans go to the polls on Wednesday, as many as 7 of 10 will vote for the African National Congress.

2004-04-14.title: Arroyo Says Philippines May Pull Out of Iraq
2004-04-14.abstract: President Gloria Macapagal Arroyo suggested today that she might withdraw Filipino military troops stationed in Iraq if the situation there deteriorates.

2004-04-15.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-04-15.abstract: MACEDONIA: Runoff Seen In Presidential Vote; THE HAGUE: Milosevic Gets A New Judge; GERMANY: SchrÖDer Blocks Book; TURKEY: Ancient Rift Is Mended;.

2004-04-15.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: New Attack On An Election Rally
2004-04-15.abstract: Suspected Islamic militants attack political rally in Banihal (Kashmir, India) just hours before planned campaign visit to region by Indian Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee; one person is killed and 22 others are wounded (S)

2004-04-15.title: For Bush and Sharon, 'Confidence' and 'Realities' Are Crucial
2004-04-15.abstract: Excerpts from White House news conference by Pres Bush and Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel (M)

2004-04-15.title: South Korea Is Wary but Firm on Iraq
2004-04-15.abstract: South Korea is training 3,000 troops to send to Iraq by June; they will join 600 already there, making South Korea's the third-largest national force in Iraq, after US and Britain; South Koreans, already divided over decision to send troops, voice stronger reservations as fighting in Iraq worsens; adding to their concerns is kidnapping and brief detention of seven South Korean missionaries there (M)

2004-04-15.title: Bin Laden Tape Offering Europe a Truce Is Called Authentic
2004-04-15.abstract: A man identifying himself as Osama bin Laden offered to stop terrorist actions in European countries that ended military action in Muslim nations.

2004-04-16.title: Bogotá Journal; For Colombia's Angry Youth, Hip-Hop Helps Keep It Real
2004-04-16.abstract: Colombia's young rap artists have seized on rap to vent about their world, which is filled with Marxist rebels, right-wing death squads, poverty and a greedy elite; their lyrics are far more political than those of rappers in most other countries; although their style is imitative of American artists, they profess disdain for their more famous counterparts in United States, who they say have sold out to get big record deals; photos (M)

2004-04-16.title: 3 Japanese Civilians Released Unharmed by Militants in Iraq
2004-04-16.abstract: Three Japanese civilian hostages in Iraq are released, ending Japanese government's first crisis in its involvement in war in Iraq; Al Jazeera network broadcast seen on Japanese television shows former captives safe in Baghdad; news of release comes with report that two other Japanese civilians may have been kidnapped (M)

2004-04-16.title: World Briefing | Europe: Cyprus: Greece Endorses Reunification
2004-04-16.abstract: Greek Prime Minister Costas Caramanlis announces his support for United Nations plan to reunify Cyprus and urges Greek Cypriots to ratify referendum (S)

2004-04-16.title: A.N.C. Again Sweeps South Africa Vote
2004-04-16.abstract: African National Congress, as expected, scores runaway victory in South Africa's general election; voter turnout, however, falls sharply from last election, raising questions about voters' satisfaction with electoral process; Democratic Alliance, party with historic ties to country's white minority, establishes itself in comparatively strong second place; photo (M)

2004-04-16.title: World Briefing | Africa: Anglican Leaders Meet About Gay Bishop
2004-04-16.abstract: Nigerian Archbishop Peter J Akinola, who leads Council of Anglican Provinces of Africa, announces group's recommendation for disciplinary action against American Episcopalians who appointed gay bishop; council vows to turn down financial aid from those congregations supporting ordination of gay bishops (S)

2004-04-17.title: Russia Objects to Measure Supporting Investigation of Oil-for-Food Program
2004-04-17.abstract: Sec Gen Kofi Annan names former Federal Reserve Chmn Paul A Volcker to lead independent panel investigating corruption in UN oil-for-food program, but objection by Russia may keep him from accepting job; UN officials say Volcker, Swiss law Prof Mark Pieth and South African Judge Richard J Goldstone were selected for panel; Russia refuses to agree to Security Council resolution that Volcker says he needs to give him authority to conduct wide-ranging inquiry (M)

2004-04-17.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Aviation Officials Sentenced In Country's Worst Crash
2004-04-17.abstract: Four Linate Airport (Milan, Italy) officials are found guilty of manslaughter and negligence for their part in 2001 runway collision that killed 118 people; accident was Italy's worst air disaster (S)

2004-04-17.title: Airport Pits Natives Against Outsiders on Japanese Isle
2004-04-17.abstract: Leaders of Ishigaki Island, tiny Japanese island in East China Sea, want to build new airport; 522 people, almost all nonlocals, are worried about environmental effects and are blocking project; they have bought tiny parcel of land allocated for airport and are refusing to sell it; battle has dragged on for generation, but with study of fourth plan nearing completion, fight may be drawing to close; photos (M)

2004-04-17.title: Impeachment Case to Go Forward in Seoul
2004-04-17.abstract: South Korea's Constitutional Court, ignoring voters' rejection of impeachment of Pres Roh Moo Hyun, rules that proceedings will go forward (S)

2004-04-17.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Bandannas Not Ruled Out
2004-04-17.abstract: French Education Minister Francois Fillon, attending meeting of Union of Islamic Organizations of France, suggests Muslim girls could wear bandanas to replace head scarf now banned in public schools (S)

2004-04-18.title: Italian Town, Lost in History, Now Fears Memories of Nazis
2004-04-18.abstract: Italian prosecutors will try three of seven former SS members in absentia for massacre committed in Tuscan town of Sant'anna di Stazzema on Aug 12, 1944; massacre occurred after Mussolini had been deposed and Italian partisans had been harassing retreating Germans; 560 women, children and elderly were rounded up, shot and their corpses burned; Alfred Schoneberg, Gerhard Sommer and Ludwig Sonntag deny any role in massacre; if convicted, they may face extradition to Italy and house arrest for life; photo; map (M)

2004-04-18.title: Terror Strike in U.S. Before Vote Is a Big Concern, Rice Says
Karzai Presides Over Signs of Economic Progress in Kabul
2004-04-18.abstract: President Hamid Karzai, putting aside concerns about persistent security problems around the nation, turned his attention to business this weekend.

2004-04-18.title: Blaming U.S., Iran Says Truce Effort in Iraq Fails
2004-04-18.abstract: Iran said today that the United States' "iron fist policy" in Iraq and a lack of security had foiled Tehran's efforts to end a stand-off in Iraq between American troops and Iraqi rebels.

2004-04-18.title: Aborigines Say Australia Pushes Their Plight to Sideline
2004-04-18.abstract: Analysts say Australian Prime Min John Howard's Conservative government has pushed needs of Aborigines to sidelines, with few complaints from white constituency; government plans to abolish elected Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Commission, which was established in 1980's as means of self-determination for indigenous people; Aborigines number 427,094 among population of more than 20 million Australians; map; photo (M)

2004-04-19.title: SPANISH PREMIER ORDERS SOLDIERS HOME FROM IRAQ
2004-04-19.abstract: Spain's new Prime Min Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero orders Spanish troops to leave Iraq 'as soon as possible,' keeping a firm campaign promise just 24 hours after he is sworn in; says it is unlikely that United Nations will be playing leading role in Iraq any time soon, which had been his condition for Spain's 1,300 troops to remain; Zapatero's move does not come as surprise to United States, but White House officials say decision is bitter moment for Pres Bush; fear is that Spanish withdrawal will make it more difficult to continue to internationalize forces in Iraq as June 30 date approaches for transfer of soverign power to Iraqi government; Spanish Defense Ministry official says Spanish withdrawal from south-central region around Najaf might take one month; national security adviser Condoleezza Rice voices fear that terrorists could draw 'wrong lesson from Spain' and attempt other attacks aimed at dividing allies; Sen John Kerry, Democrat running for president, expresses regret at Zapatero's decision, noting need for United States to find new coalition partners to share burden in Iraq; photo; chart (M)

2004-04-19.title: A pivotal role envisaged on EU's southern flank
2004-04-19.abstract: VALLETTA, Malta—  The infant Jesus peers down from a large oil painting in the office of Prime Minister Lawrence Gonzi, a fitting tableau for a country that is literally a fortress of Catholicism in the southern Mediterranean.

2004-04-19.title: Names of the Dead
2004-04-19.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of service member in Iraq (S)

2004-04-19.title: Beirut Journal; On Game Show, Arab Drumbeat: Remember Jerusalem
2004-04-19.abstract: Game show running on Hezbollah's satellite television channel Al Manar, broadcast from Lebanon, offers novel way for Hezbollah to promote its theme--that all Arab efforts should be concentrated on reconquering land lost to Israel, especially Jerusalem; game show, called The Mission, follows standard game show format, but at least half the questions revolve around Palestinian or Islamic history, and at least one contestant is usually Palestinian; critics suspect taht Hezbollah's backers, Iran and Syria, are using relatively free speech of Lebanon to promote anti-European, anti-American, anti-semitic hatreds they would not dare pronounce at home; photo; map (M)

2004-04-19.title: Bremer Raising Pressure to End Iraqi Uprisings
2004-04-19.abstract: American civil administrator L Paul Bremer III warns of military showdown in Falluja and Najaf because of rebels' refusal to submit to American demands; speaks of need to bring early end to standoffs in Najaf with followers of Moktada al-Sadr, and in Falluja with Sunni Muslim insurgents; uprisings have posed greatest challenge to Amerian authority since invasion; American troops have been held back in Falluja and Najaf to allow mediation, but American generals say they will use force if talks drag on without result; until now, Bremer has said little publicly about crisis but has reportedly been working intensively behind scenes to allay impatience within American military command over standoffs and to give Iraqi negotiators as much time as possible to find peaceful solution; photo (M)

2004-04-20.title: Names of the Dead
2004-04-20.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of seven service membbers in Iraq, bringing total to 690 (S)

2004-04-20.title: U.S. Pressure to Hold Militant Sets Off Outcry in Indonesia
2004-04-20.abstract: Bush administration seeks to ensure that Abu Bakar Bashir, leader of militant Islamic organization Jemaah Islamiyah, remains in jail in Jakarta, Indonesia, and is fully prosecuted on terrorism charges; effort sets off diplomatic and political tempest in Indonesia; Islamists and nationalists denounce what they see as undue interference in Indonesia's internal affairs; Bashir was convicted of only relatively minor offenses last Sept and Supreme Court ruling makes him eligible for release; Australia joins US in opposing his release; photo (M)

2004-04-20.title: JORDANIAN KING PUTS OFF MEETING BUSH OVER ISRAEL
2004-04-20.abstract: King Abdullah of Jordan puts off visit to Washington, in rebuff to Pres Bush linked to his recent support for Israel's territorial claims in West Bank; Jordanian government issues statement saying meeting will wait until Bush administration clarifies American position on peace process and final situation in Palestinian territories; Jordanian officials make clear that Abdullah is irked by Bush's declarations in his meeting with Prime Min Ariel Sharon last week, during which Bush broke with American policy and supported Israel's ultimate retention of some settlements in West Bank and also rejected longtime Palestinian demand for right of return to homes abandoned in 1948 in what is now Israel; Jordanian statement follows by week similar protest by Pres Hosni Mubarak of Egypt; administration officials say that they still expect Jordan and Egypt to try to help with security in Gaza Strip and parts of West Bank, from which Israeli forces and settlers are to be withdrawn under Sharon's plan (M)

2004-04-20.title: United Nations Journal; At East River Landmark, Creaking Old Bones Are Bared
2004-04-20.abstract: John M Clarkson, program director for United Nations' master plan to renovate aging interior of UN headquarters building, offers 'dirty tour' of landmark marble and glass tower, focusing on its eyesores and decay; master plan to gut and refurbish emptied headquarters complex--subject to approval of $1.2 billion loan proopsed in Pres Bush's 2005 budget--is to finish in time for staff members to return in 2011; they will be relocatted temporarily in 2007 to 35-story building under construction nearby; photos (M)

2004-04-20.title: Truce Lets Families Returnto Falluja, a Few at a Time
2004-04-20.abstract: A negotiated peace got off to a slow, and sometimes chaotic, start today in the embattled city of Falluja.

2004-04-21.title: U.S. Generals Fault Ban on Hussein's Party
2004-04-21.abstract: Maj Gen John R S Batiste and Brig Gen Carter F Ham warn that blanket exclusion of former Baath Party members, including senior army officers, from government jobs is depriving Iraq of skilled professionals and breeding resentment among disenfranchised Sunnis; say cases should be considered individually, since many only belonged to party to work; photo; US administrator Paul Bremer's attempt to ease restrictions reportedly met with furious response from Iraqi Governing Council, especially Ahmed Chalabi, who has close Pentagon ties; Sen Carl Levin notes that UN envoy Lakhdar Brahimi also believes excluded professionals are sorely needed (M)

2004-04-21.title: Names of the Dead
2004-04-21.abstract: List of US soldiers newly confirmed dead in Iraq; 695 have died since start of war

2004-04-21.title: U.S. Generals Criticize
2004-04-21.abstract: Comment by two American generals that former Iraqi Baath Party members, including senior Army officers, should be accepted for government jobs noted

2004-04-21.title: Kim in Beijing But Nowhere To Be Seen
2004-04-21.abstract: North Korean leader Kim Jong Il spends his second day in Beijing somewhere in hiding, even as Chinese government receives Cambodian leader Hun Sen with full official ceremonies; South Korean journalists break news of Kim Jong Il's train trip into China; Chinese media ignores visit (M)

2004-04-21.title: With Eye on Europe, Turkey Backs Bid to Reunite Cyprus
2004-04-21.abstract: Turkey, intent on winning points with European Union, throws full weight behind UN proposal to reunite Cyprus, despite domestic charges of betraying island's Turkish population; US and European officials praise decision; Greek and Turkish Cypriotes still have to decide in separate referendums whether to live under loose federation after thirty years of strict, bitter division (M)

2004-04-22.title: Four Killed and 148 Wounded In a Suicide Bombing in Riyadh
2004-04-22.abstract: Suicide bomber detonates car loaded with explosives in central Riyadh, killing at least four people, wounding 148 and shattering facade of police building; blast is felt three miles away; Saudi officials and foreign embassies have been bracing for an attack since Apr 12, when shootout in poor Riyadh neighborhood led to chain of bloody skirmishes across central part of kingdom; Saudi officials say bombing and clashes preceding it represent qualitative shift in ongoing war between security services and Islamic militants believed to be either directly connected to Osama bin Laden and Al Qaeda or inspired by terrorist group; analysts say success of security services in arresting militants over past year has disrupted their ability to mount organized effort to shake ruling Saud familty, but it has also made situation more volatile as those determined to carry out suicide attacks may pick almost any random target; Deputy Secretary of State Richard L Armitage, who was in Riyadh when bombing occurred, says two governments have been sharing counterterrorism intelligence, which is why American Embassy ordered many staff members and their families to leave country; photos; map (M)

2004-04-22.title: Fighting Resumes in Falluja; Return of Families Is Halted
2004-04-22.abstract: Insurgents in Falluja fire mortars and battle with marines, threatening fragile peace effort and forcing American military to halt return of families who had fled fighting; roughly third of city residents are said to have fled; at checkpoint into city there is no sign of Iraqi police who were expected to man checkpoint and vet families; effort to persuade insurgents to hand over their heavy weapons fares poorly; photo of heavy weapons surrendered, which marine describes as 'not serviceable...junk' (M)

2004-04-22.title: 9 Palestinians Killed by Israelis In Second Day of Gaza Fighting
2004-04-22.abstract: Israeli troops in armored vehicles kill 9 Palestinians and wound about 25 in second day of running clashes in northern Gaza Strip; Israeli operation seeks to stop Palestinian rocket fire, which intensified after Israelis killed Hamas leader Abdel Aziz Rantisi (M)

2004-04-22.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Tamale Vendor Arrested And Food Checked For Human Remains
2004-04-22.abstract: Michoacan (Mexico) police arrest tamale vendor and test meat for human remains after multilated body is found in vendor's home (S)

2004-04-22.title: Hope Intermingles With Grief Among Halliburton Workers
2004-04-22.abstract: Fate of four Halliburton contract workers missing in Iraq since Apr 9 remains unclear, day after bodies of three others ambushed in same attack are identified; Halliburton and its subsidiary Kellogg Brown & Root issue statement mourning loss of three workers; company has more than 24,000 employees and subcontractors in Iraq and Kuwait; 32 have been killed since conflict began; of four still missing, only one, Thomas Hamill, has been identified because he was seen in video released by his captors; Pfc Keith M Maupin has also been seen in video broadcast by Al Jazeera; Sgt Elmer C Krause is also listed as missing (M)

2004-04-23.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Iconic 'Guernica Oak' Dies
2004-04-23.abstract: Oak tree in Guernica, long a symbol of Basque identity, dies as consequence of heat wave in summer of 2003 (S)

2004-04-23.title: Freed From Captivity in Iraq, Japanese Return to More Pain
2004-04-23.abstract: Japanese civilians taken hostage in Iraq are reviled on their return to Japan and have gone into hiding, effectively becoming prisoners inside their own homes; are accused of acting selfishly in pursuing their individual goals, defying government advisory against travel to Iraq and causing trouble for Japan; government says it will bill them $6,000 for their return air fare; former hostages--Nahoko Takato, Noriaki Imai, Soichiro Koriyama, Junpei Yasuda and Nobutaka Watanabe--are nevertheless praised by Sec of State Colin L Powell; photos (M)

2004-04-23.title: Names of the Dead
2004-04-23.abstract: Defense Department confirms death of three more American service members in Iraq war, bringing total to 698

2004-04-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: Bomb Explodes In Kandahar
2004-04-23.abstract: Bomb explodes in Kandahar, Afghanistan, perhaps signaling growing urban bombing campaign by Taliban (S)

2004-04-23.title: Rumsfeld Era at Pentagon May Be Prolonged
2004-04-23.abstract: What will President Bush's national security team look like if the president manages to defeat his Democratic challenger in November?

2004-04-24.title: U.N. Distances Itself From an Envoy's Rebuke of Israel and the U.S.
2004-04-24.abstract: United Nations officials move to disassociate organization from damning remarks about Israel and US policy in Middle East made by Lakhdar Brahimi, its special envoy to Iraq (M)

2004-04-24.title: A 'Bone Woman' Chronicles the World's Massacres
2004-04-24.abstract: Biographical profile of Clea Koff, forensic anthropologist who helped provide critical evidence for international trials of authorities in Rwanda and Bosnia who have been charged with crimes against humanity and genocide; Koff has chronicled her experiences unearthing mass graves in book Bone Woman, published to coincide with 10th anniversary of genocide in Rwanda that left 800,000 dead; her photo (M)

2004-04-24.title: General Says He May Ask for More Troops
2004-04-24.abstract: Gen John P Abizaid, top US commander in Middle East, says he may ask for another extension in current troop levels in Iraq, currently at 135,000, and may even ask for more troops beyond that; Abizaid photo (M)

2004-04-24.title: World Briefing | Americas: Venezuela: In Poll, Chávez Wins Recall
2004-04-24.abstract: Alfredo Keller & Associates public opinion poll holds that if Venezuelan President Hugo Chavez is recalled he will win referendum and stay in office; opposition is still deciding whether to recheck signatures and go forward with recall (S)

2004-04-24.title: Rights Group Says Sudan's Government Aided Militias' Raids
2004-04-24.abstract: Human Rights Watch report accuses Arab-led Sudanese government of joining Arab militias in attacks on black Africans in Darfur region, clearing villages, destroying their food supplies and executing men deemed enemies; United Nations Human Rights Commission passes resolution on abuses in Darfur that US rejects as too soft on Sudan (M)

2004-04-25.title: Redefined Boundaries and Uneasy Transformations; Immigration Matters Raise New Concerns For Czech Republic
2004-04-25.abstract: Third article in series The New Europe examines immigration issues facing expanded European Union; new members in Eastern Europe face demographic problem of aging populations and shrinking workforce and could become magnets for legal and illegal immigration, a marked shift for societies closed off for 40 years under Communism; International Organization for Migration identifies Poland, Czech Republic, Slovakia and Slovenia as attractive destinations; debate over immigration in Czech Republic, which has nearly 230,000 legal immigrants, described; photos; map (M)

2004-04-25.title: Spain's New Premier Pays Visit to Morocco
2004-04-25.abstract: Spain's new Prime Min Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero visits Morocco for talks; hopes to repair strained relations of last few years and deepen cooperation between countries in struggle against terrorism and control of illegal immigration; plans to lobby for greater European political and economic support for Morocco; will continue policy of promoting Spanish private investment in Morocco; photo (M)

2004-04-25.title: Redefined Boundaries and Uneasy Transformations; Poland Is Worried That Border Controls Create a New Divide
2004-04-25.abstract: Fourth article in series The New Europe examines Poland's concern that insistence of current European Union member states on tightly controlled eastern border as prerequisite for Polish membership will create new economic and ideological divide; Western Europeans worry about being flooded by low-cost labor from east; several countries have announced restrictions on job seekers from new states for several years; most of Poland's new security in East is being funded by Western countries (M)

2004-04-25.title: Suited to Guerrillas, a Dusty Town Poses Tricky Perils
2004-04-25.abstract: Contemplated Marine invasion of Falluja would face all the perils of urban warfare; city is complex mix of boulevards, narrow streets and back alleys lined with low buildings, ideally suited to urban guerrillas; high casualties among city's 300,000 residents or damage to mosques, hospitals and schools would likely anger Iraqis; military hopes new tactics will allow troops to avoid protracted block-to-block fight with estimated 2,000 hard-core insurgents in city, including some 200 foreign fighters; photo; maps (M)

2004-04-25.title: Marines in Falluja Face Rebels Who Are Increasingly Trained and Armed
2004-04-25.abstract: United States Marines preparing for all-out assault on Falluja have unexpectedly become locked in one of fiercest and costliest battles of war; marines have throw cordon around city and have conducted weeks of street fighting against enemies who have hidden stores of arms and, sometimes, military sophistication; believe they face variety of forces, including Baath party loyalists, former military men, foreign jihadists, thugs and excitable young people (M)

2004-04-26.title: Names of the Dead
2004-04-26.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of two service members in Iraq, bringing death toll to 701 since war in Iraq began (S)

2004-04-26.title: French Rightist Stirs Up a Storm Visiting Britain
2004-04-26.abstract: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader, visits Manchester, Eng, to offer support for white supremacist British National Party; protesters gather, throwing eggs and garbage to protest his appearance; both Le Pen and British rightists seek following among those who blame immigrants for many of their country's woes (M)

2004-04-26.title: A 2nd Term? Colombians Deeply Split
2004-04-26.abstract: Colombians are deeply split over bill that would permit Pres Alvaro Uribe to have second term; Uribe is immensely popular; if bill is passed and there were an election, he would probably win; but Colombians wonder whether sitting president, even one so deeply admired, should lead effort that would clearly benefit his own government; his quest for second term, even if successful, could have pitfalls; to win enough suport in Congress he would have to give opposition politicians important government jobs and support their pet projects, thus weakening his carefully cultivated image as independent (M)

2004-04-26.title: Attack in Iraq: Many Versions, Obscure Truth
2004-04-26.abstract: Iraqis and American military offer conflicting accounts of roadside bombing of American Humvee in Baghdad that killed one soldier, and its aftermath; after dead soldier is evacuated, Humvee is set on fire and children rush out to celebrate attack; American soldiers begin shooting at rooftop snipers and at least one Iraqi teen-ager is killed in cross-fire; Iraqi witnesses claim five children were killed by American soldiers, but make no mention of gunmen firing from rooftops; several Iraqis say children had been incited to jump around burning Humvee by cameraman for Al Arabiya, Arab news channel which American officials blame for stoking anti-Americanism among viewers around Arab world; station denies charge; contradictory versions of what happened are revealing about thickening fog of this war; many Western reporters are mostly ensconced in hotels and houses protected by huge concrete blast walls, because of recent wave of kidnappings and killings of foreigners; photo (M)

2004-04-26.title: Militants in Europe Openly Call For Jihad and the Rule of Islam
2004-04-26.abstract: Hundreds of young Muslim men in Europe are thought to be answering call to jihad of militant groups affiliated with Al Qaeda; swear allegiance to Osama bin Laden and his goal of toppling Western democracies to establish Islamic superstate under Shariah law, like Afghanistan under Taliban; they call Sept 11 hijackers 'Magnificent 19' and regard Madrid train bombings as clever way to drive wedge into Europe; in London, Sheik Omar Bakri Mohammad echoes bin Laden's warning that Muslims will attack any European country that does not withdraw its troops from Iraq in next three months; intelligence officials say fervor for militancy is intensifying and becoming more open in old industrial towns of England, and in Arab enclaves of Germany, France, Switzerland and other parts of Europe; say level of 'chatter'--communications among people suspected of terrorism and their supporters--has markedly increased since bin Laden's warning to Europe this month; counterterrorism officials say some Muslim recruits are going to Iraq, but more are remaning home, possibly joining cells that could help with terror logistics or begin operations like one that came to notice when British police seized 1,200 pounds of ammonium nitrate in late Mar and arrested nine Pakistani-Britons, charging five of them with trying to build terrorist bomb; photos; map (M)

2004-04-27.title: U.N.'s Envoy for Iraq Takes the Spotlight
2004-04-27.abstract: Lakhdar Brahimi, United Nations' special envoy to Baghdad, will make much anticipated report to Security Council about how to best create transitional authority for Iraq; is expected to call for caretaker government with president and two vice presidents and prime minister and consultative conference to work alongside provisional administration until elections in June 2005; is expected to call for dissolution of American-appointed Iraqi Governing Council, one of whose members, Ahmad Chalabi, has denounced Brahimi as having 'Arab nationalist agenda' that could prove divisive in Iraq; photo (M)

2004-04-27.title: World Briefing | Asia: Afghanistan: Nato To Widen Role
2004-04-27.abstract: North Atlantic Treaty Organization announces plans to expand presence in Afghanistan to included at least five more reconstruction teams in north (S)

2004-04-27.title: Citing `Crunch Point,' Blair Plans Review of Immigration Rules
2004-04-27.abstract: The announcement by British Prime Minister Tony Blair came just four days before the enlargement of the European Union removes obstacles to continental migration.

2004-04-27.title: China Expands Quarantine in Aggressive Effort to Contain SARS
2004-04-27.abstract: Chinese government continues its aggressive efforts to stamp out new SARS outbreak by increasing number of people under quarantine to nearly 500 and dispatching special medical teams to review government laboratories that work with deadly virus; government is also increasing health screenings at airports and train stations, hoping to prevent wider spread of disease before millions of people begin traveling for May Day holiday celebration (M)

2004-04-27.title: Qaddafi Makes First Visit to Europe in 15 Years
2004-04-27.abstract: The Libyan leader paraded into the heart of Europe today to announce his country's return from the diplomatic outback.

2004-04-28.title: China Bristles At Criticism Of Hong Kong Restrictions
2004-04-28.abstract: Chinese Foreign Min Li Zhaoxing responds angrily to US and British criticisms of China's latest restrictions on democracy in Hong Kong, charging double standard by two countries that showed little such concern during more than century of British rule (M)

2004-04-28.title: Israelis March In Gaza Strip, 70,000 Strong
2004-04-28.abstract: Tens of thousands of Israelis march in seaside settlement of Gush Katif on Israel's Independence Day to protest Prime Min Ariel Sharon's plan to withdraw settlers and soldiers from Gaza Strip; photo; many of estimated 70,000 come from West Bank settlements; three Palestinians die in Gaza when their suicide bomb explodes; Israeli soldiers kill two Hamas fighters in Tulkarm (M)

2004-04-28.title: Thai Security Forces Kill Scores of Rebels
2004-04-28.abstract: At least 104 people were killed today when government security forces repelled coordinated predawn attacks by machete-wielding teens.

2004-04-28.title: Damascus Hit By a Bombing And a Gunfight
2004-04-28.abstract: Heavily armed assailants detonate bomb near cluster of foreign embassies in Damascus, setting off intense gun battle with Syrian security forces; officials blame 'terrorist and sabotage group,' linking attacks to those in other Arab capitals; one officer and woman passing by are reported killed, as well as two attackers; weapons cache is found in raid in upscale Mazza district; photo of damage to building housing UN office; map (M)

2004-04-28.title: With Skeptical Eye, Marines Train Iraqis for Falluja Patrols
2004-04-28.abstract: Iraqi troops in Falluja practice storming a building under watchful, if dubious, eyes of US marines they are expected to join on patrols in violent city; photo; marines themselves are calling plan 'Suicide Patrol' (S)

2004-04-29.title: Ban on Weapons of Doom Is Extended to Qaeda-Style Groups
2004-04-29.abstract: Security Council unanimously approves resolution that extends reach of chemical, biological and nuclear weapons non-proliferation treaty beyond states alone to 'non-state actors'--diplomatic language for terror groups; this was first proposed in United Nations General Assembly speech by Pres Bush in Sept; 15-to-0 vote follows months of negotiating and redrafting to win over one reluctant permanent member--China, which ended threat to use its veto when language is dropped that called for interception of ships at sea suspected of carrying banned weapons; no specific enforcement power is included in resolution, although UN charter rules allow for sanctions against non-cooperating countries (M)

2004-04-29.title: World Briefing | Asia: Sri Lanka: Opponents Of Peace Talks Join Cabinet
2004-04-29.abstract: Sri Lankan President Chandrika Kumaratunga forms coalition cabinet that has four ministers from Sir Lanka Peoples Liberation Front, which is opposed to current peace talks with Tamil rebels (S)

2004-04-29.title: Deal to End Falluja Standoff Takes Shape; 10 Americans Die
2004-04-29.abstract: Under the tentative plan, ex-Iraqi soldiers and officers will replace U.S. forces encircling Falluja and try to pacify rebels.

2004-04-29.title: U.S. Forces Rush to Send Tanks to Iraq
2004-04-29.abstract: Army and Marine Corps are rushing dozens of 70-ton battle tanks and armored personnel carriers to Iraq to help meet rising security threat to American troops; commanders' urgent requests for armor underscore how military misjudged scope and ferocity of recent insurgent attacks, which have killed at least 115 American troops in Apr; many died when roadside bombs ripped through thin-skinned Humvees (M)

2004-04-29.title: World Briefing | Africa: South Africa: Minister Who Balked On AIDS Is Reappointed
2004-04-29.abstract: South African President Thabo Mbeki reappoints Health Minister Manto Tshabalala-Msimang despite concerns of critics who say she mishandled AIDS crisis by suggesting HIV can be treated with garlic and beet root (S)

2004-04-30.title: France Is Struggling to Suppress Extremist Muslim Clerics
2004-04-30.abstract: Abdelkadar Bouziane is fifth Muslim cleric expelled from France this year on charges of spreading dangerously divisive brand of radical Islam; France has one of strictest antiterrorism programs in Europe, because it was hit early by Islamist terror and because it has largest Muslim population on Continent; making its hard-line stance stick is difficult, even in country more willing than most of its neighbors to limit free speech in interest of calm and cohesive society; Bouziane has won appeal that would allow him to return to France, despite court evidence showing his links to groups supporting terrorism; his expulsion and possible return highlight thorny issue most countries across Europe face as they struggle to meet needs of growing Muslim populations; part of problem is dearth of domestically trained clerics to lead congregations of European-born Muslims; photos; map (M)

2004-04-30.title: Siyu Journal; Why a Village Well Is a Weapon in the War on Terror
2004-04-30.abstract: American military is undertaking many public works projects throughout Horn of Arfrica as part of war on terrorism, with aim of changing locals' view of United States, country many of them have learned to hate; one such project is new well for remote fishing village of Siyu, Kenya, where investigators say several terrorist acts were planned in 2002, including bombing of Mombasa hotel; say island was perfect hide-out for enterprising terrorist like Fazul Abdullah Mohammed, chief suspect in Mombasa attacks and also wanted in connection with 1998 bombing of American Embassy in Nairobi; Mohammed recruited two of his in-laws in Siyu into his Al Qaeda operation; both of them are standing trial while Mohammed has disappeared; photos; map (M)

2004-04-30.title: A Full Range of Technology Is Applied to Bomb Falluja
2004-04-30.abstract: Military officials say airstrikes in Falluja in past three days by American warplanes and helicopter gunships have been most intense aerial bombardment in Iraq since major combat ended nearly year ago; commanders say they go to great lengths to avoid civilian casualties, but acknowledge they do not know how many civilians have died in recent attacks; pilots concede that in at least one case, American warplanes mistakenly bombed wrong building; bloodbath in Falluja, if it occurs, might return city to American control, only to galvanize resistance around country (M)

2004-04-30.title: U.N. Struggles to Find Troops to Police Haiti
2004-04-30.abstract: United Nations is having hard time signing up peacekeeping troops and French-speaking police officers to take over security in Haiti from American-led interim force; diplomats express concern that large parts of country remain under rebels' control, and some potential contributors are reluctant to offer troops because of lingering doubts about conditions of Pres Jean-Bertrand Aristide's departure; 15 nations of Caribbean Community have not ruled out role in new security forces but are deferring decision on recognizing Haiti's government until July (M)

2004-04-30.title: World Briefing | Europe: Macedonia: Pro-Westerner Elected President
2004-04-30.abstract: Macedonian Prime Minister Branko Crvenkovski wins Presidential election with 62 percent of vote, defeating conservative rival Sasko Kedev; election was called after death of President Boris Trajkovski in February 2004 plane crash; photo (S)
